channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Lula enaltece diálogo com prefeitos e ataca as notícias falsas
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva emphasized dialogue with municipal mayors in an interview with TV Record Bahia, stating that no government in Brazil's history has listened to local officials as much as his current administration. He also attacked the spread of fake news, defending the importance of truth in public discourse.

item.title: Não acaba em pizza
item.description: The Rome Court of Appeal rejected the defense arguments of former deputy Carla Zambelli, which were based on disinformation, and approved her extradition to Brazil. Zambelli was sentenced by the STF to ten years in prison for invading the CNJ’s electronic system in partnership with hacker Walter Delgatti during the 2022 campaign to cast doubt on election integrity. After nine months in Italian custody awaiting the ruling, her lawyers plan to appeal to the Court of Cassation, but European jurists and Brazilian authorities view the decision as a legal landmark.

item.title: As coisas como elas são
item.description: Gisele Agnelli's 'Autocracia Made in USA' examines the second Trump administration as a real-time autocratic project, documenting the erosion of U.S. democratic institutions through 2025 columns and interviews. As a Latin American immigrant political scientist, she offers a sharp comparative lens, linking U.S. democratic backsliding to Brazil's own political crises.

item.title: Venha quem vier
item.description: Edinho Silva, president of the Workers' Party (PT), states that Flávio Bolsonaro's rise in polls was expected due to polarization and the weight of the Bolsonaro surname. He denies the PT has a preference for specific opponents, downplays Ronaldo Caiado's impact, and argues that Lula's 2026 campaign will present a modern, forward-looking program aimed at younger generations, expecting a shift in voter sentiment by mid-year.

item.title: Moraes aumenta área de restrição de drones no entorno da residência de Bolsonaro
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes expanded the drone no-fly zone to a 1-kilometer radius around Jair Bolsonaro's residence in Brasília, authorizing the Federal District Military Police to shoot down and seize violating drones, with operators facing arrest in flagrante. The measure aims to prevent unauthorized surveillance and protect privacy, while Bolsonaro, sentenced to 27 years and three months for attempting a coup, awaits a ruling on house arrest due to hospitalization, under conditions including electronic monitoring and bans on cell phones and social media.

item.title: STF/ Oásis ou miragem?
item.description: Minister Flávio Dino of the STF ordered a preliminary investigation into possible irregularities in the allocation of R$ 3.6 million in amendments by Senator Carlos Viana to the Fundação Oásis, linked to the Lagoinha Baptist Church, citing 'lack of transparency and traceability'. The case may be referred to the Federal Police if evidence of purpose diversion or favoritism is found. In a separate matter, state deputy Fabiana Bolsonaro (PL-SP) is under criminal investigation by the MPF for blackface and transphobia after a speech in the São Paulo State Assembly questioning the legitimacy of trans deputy Érika Hilton as head of the Women's Rights Defense Committee.

item.title: Ao vivo: Lula, Rui Costa e Jerônimo visitam obras do VLT de Salvador
item.description: President Lula will visit the VLT construction site in Salvador on Thursday, April 2, 2026, alongside Governor Jerônimo Rodrigues and Minister Rui Costa. The event will include the signing of the service order for Line 1 of the metro, a R$1.52 billion investment under the Novo PAC.

item.title: A palavra de ordem na campanha de Caiado em relação a Lula e Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: In Ronaldo Caiado's campaign, the political strategy draws a distinction between adversaries and competitors: Lula is framed as an ideological adversary, while Flávio Bolsonaro is seen as a direct competitor for the same conservative and right-wing electorate. This differentiation suggests Caiado aims to position himself as an alternative to Bolsonarism without directly attacking its leader, focusing on capturing Bolsonaro's voter base while maintaining clear opposition to the PT. The message reflects an attempt to navigate the polarized landscape with electoral tact.

item.title: TSE julgará recurso de deputado da motosserra na próxima 5ª feira
item.description: The TSE will rule on Thursday, April 9, 2026, on an appeal by Hildebrando Pascoal Nogueira Neto, known as the 'chainsaw deputy', seeking to overturn his 14-year electoral conviction for corruption and criminal association, finalized in 2004. He has been imprisoned since 1999 for leading a death squad in Acre responsible for murders, torture, and dismemberment with a chainsaw, including the 1996 killing of mechanic Agilson Firmino and his 13-year-old son. The appeal, filed as a regimental challenge, contests the monocratic decision of Minister André Ramos Tavares that denied the criminal revision, with rapporteur Minister Estela Aranha.

item.title: Após PSD lançar pré-candidatura de Caiado, senadora deixa o partido e se filia ao PT
item.description: Senator Eliziane Gama left the PSD and joined the PT following the launch of Ronaldo Caiado’s presidential pre-candidacy, which repositioned the party as an alternative to Lula’s government. Her departure is part of a broader PSD realignment, after Rodrigo Pacheco also left for the PSB. As a key ally of the government and advocate for Jorge Messias’ STF nomination, Eliziane strengthens Lula’s coalition in Maranhão and supports the pre-candidacy of Vice Governor Felipe Camarão.

item.title: Moraes amplia para 1 km restrição a drones sobre casa de Bolsonaro
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes expanded the drone restriction radius around former President Jair Bolsonaro’s residence from 100 meters to 1 kilometer, approving a request from the Federal District Military Police. The decision, issued on Thursday (April 2), was based on a technical assessment concluding the previous limit was inadequate to ensure the protective measure’s effectiveness, given drones’ ability to capture images from greater distances.

item.title: Terceira via à moda brasileira
item.description: The text critiques the notion that candidates like Caiado, Zema, or Aldo Rebelo represent a 'Third Way' in Brazil's 2026 elections, arguing they are aligned with reactionary, anarcho-liberal, or nationalist right-wing ideologies. It asserts that the true Third Way—historically a moderate path between extreme neoliberalism and excessive welfare statism—is today embodied by the PT and PSB, which form the governing coalition, and notes that the PSDB once held this profile. It concludes that labeling third-party candidates as a 'Third Way' is an illusion, as they do not offer a genuine alternative to the polarized Lula vs. bolsonarismo dynamic.

item.title: Após lançamento de candidatura de Caiado à Presidência, senadora Eliziane Gama troca PSD pelo PT
item.description: Senator Eliziane Gama (MA) left the PSD and joined the PT after Ronaldo Caiado was officially nominated as the party's presidential candidate. She announced the decision on Instagram, stating that while she respects the PSD's new direction, her thinking differs from the party's. Caiado's candidacy has been largely avoided by PSD governors, with only Ratinho Júnior initially offering public support, while Eduardo Leite initially criticized the move before moderating his stance.

item.title: Paralisa ou endividamento? O que explica queda na popularidade do governo
item.description: The decline in President Lula’s popularity is linked to rising household debt, with half of Brazilian families indebted and 82 million in default, according to the Central Bank and Serasa. Economist Alex Agostini argues that the lack of structural fiscal adjustment is forcing consumption to rely on credit rather than income, worsened by economic slowdown and low job creation, a trend expected to persist through 2026.

item.title: Eleições 2026: saiba até quando e como tirar, regularizar ou transferir o título de eleitor
item.description: The deadline to obtain, regularize, or transfer a voter title for the 2026 elections is May 6. After this date, the TSE will definitively close the electoral roll, with general elections scheduled for October 4. Voter registration and voting are optional for 16- and 17-year-olds, mandatory from age 18, and 15-year-olds who turn 16 by election day may already obtain their title.

item.title: Lula anulará leilão da Petrobras que vendeu gás de cozinha mais caro
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva announced he will annul Petrobras' auctions that sold cooking gas (LPG) at prices above the official table, held on March 31, calling them 'cretinous' and 'criminal'. He stated the auctions were conducted against the state company's management direction and that the poor will not pay for the war in Iran. The sale of 70,000 tons of LPG, equivalent to 11% of April's projected national sales, could add up to R$ 39,40 per 13 kg cylinder, affecting the Gás do Povo program.

item.title: Comitê da Câmara dos EUA acusa Moraes de ‘censura’ e cita eleições de 2026 no Brasil
item.description: The U.S. House Judiciary Committee released a report accusing Brazilian Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes of leading a censorship regime, including global content removal orders and partnerships with foreign censors, claiming it threatens American free speech. The report also ties Moraes’ actions to the 2026 Brazilian presidential election, suggesting bias in favor of Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva in a potential runoff against Flávio Bolsonaro.

item.title: JOTA Principal: Messias volta à pauta e, de novo, testa ruído persistente entre Lula e Alcolumbre
item.description: Allied senators are urging Senate President Davi Alcolumbre to advance Jorge Messias’ nomination to the Supreme Federal Court, but Alcolumbre continues to express dissatisfaction with the Lula government, seeking to inflict a political defeat on the president. Although government and opposition sources believe Messias has the 41 votes needed for approval, Alcolumbre has not attended the session and insists on a personal meeting with Lula before proceeding, preserving the ongoing tension between the Executive and Legislative branches.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro diz que “viver no Brasil está caro” e cobra redução de impostos
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) stated that "living in Brazil is expensive" and called for tax reductions to alleviate the cost of living for the population. The legislator, who presents himself as a pre-candidate for the Presidency, criticized official inflation indices and advocated for measures to reduce the tax burden as a way to improve Brazilians' purchasing power.

item.title: Preocupado com combustível, Lula contraria Trump sobre fim da guerra no Irã
item.description: President Lula contradicted Donald Trump's claim that the war in Iran is nearing its end, stating the conflict will not end quickly and expressing concern over its impact on fuel prices in Brazil. He warned that rising fuel costs are hurting ordinary Brazilians, especially truckers and families, and accused fuel distributors of illegally profiting despite the government's reduction of PIS and Cofins taxes. Lula announced a serious investigation by federal police to target abusive pricing practices.

item.title: Lava Jato ainda assombra Lula e PT
item.description: Operation Car Wash was dismantled by the Supreme Federal Court (STF) through the annulment of Lula's convictions, formally rehabilitating politicians such as Sérgio Cabral and Eduardo Cunha, some of whom are running in the 2026 elections. Despite the formal annulment, the crimes uncovered by the investigation remain a political burden for Lula and the PT, who continue to be associated with the scandal in public opinion.

item.title: Lula diz que pediu a Donald Trump a prisão do ‘dono da Refit’
item.description: President Lula stated in an interview with TV Record Bahia that he requested U.S. President Donald Trump to arrest Brazilian businessman Ricardo Magro, owner of Grupo Refit, whom he identified as a key figure in corruption schemes. Lula emphasized the goal of targeting high-level corruption by focusing on wealthy individuals living in affluent areas of major Brazilian cities and urged U.S. cooperation in extraditing fugitive Brazilians residing in the United States.

item.title: Crusoé: “Vocês são as pessoas honestas que querem que eu seja”, diz Lula
item.description: During the inauguration of the ITA's new facilities in Ceará, President Lula (PT) praised his supporters and students present, stating they are 'the honest people I want to be'. He urged them not to give up on politics, emphasizing youth engagement amid widespread misinformation on social media.

item.title: Governo Lula pagou menos de 1% das emendas obrigatórias previstas na LDO
item.description: The Lula government has paid only 0.6% of the mandatory parliamentary amendments outlined in the LDO for the first half of 2026, amounting to R$102.3 million out of a projected R$17.3 billion. The low disbursement raises concerns about budgetary compliance and executive-legislative relations.

item.title: Lula reforça criação do Ministério da Segurança Pública: 'Estamos em guerra contra o crime organizado'
item.description: President Lula da Silva urged Congress to approve the Public Security PEC to establish a new Ministry of Public Security, stating Brazil is now in a 'war against organized crime'. The initiative aims to integrate security forces and expand federal authority following the enactment of the Anti-Crime Law in March. Opposition governors resist, fearing loss of state autonomy, while the government views the proposal as politically strategic to bolster Lula’s 2026 re-election bid amid rising voter concern over crime.

item.title: 'Estamos fazendo tudo possível para que a guerra irresponsável do Irã não chegue ao povo', diz Lula
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that the government is doing everything possible to prevent Iran’s irresponsible war from affecting the Brazilian people, especially to avoid hitting the wallets of truckers and housewives. He also commented on the Security PEC, saying that once approved by the Senate, it will allow the creation of a thematic ministry to fight organized crime without needing permission from anyone. Lula reiterated Brazil’s willingness to cooperate with the United States against organized crime, mentioning the extradition of fugitives such as Ricardo Magro.

item.title: Lula quer prender empresário, Petrobras impõe aumento de 55% à aviação
item.description: Lula threatens to jail fuel distributors for allegedly inflating prices at the pump despite state subsidies, while Petrobras announced a 55% increase in aviation kerosene prices, raising costs for airlines and threatening higher fares and reduced services. The rhetoric comes amid global oil price surges linked to Middle East conflicts and aims to shift blame from Petrobras to private distributors.

item.title: PL monta plano com Flávio e Nikolas para vencer eleição no RJ, se pleito for no voto direto
item.description: The PL is preparing a plan for a direct election in Rio de Janeiro, should the STF rule that the interim mandate be decided by popular vote rather than indirectly by the Legislative Assembly. The strategy involves deploying Nikolas Ferreira and Flávio Bolsonaro as key electoral allies for deputy Douglas Ruas to boost his visibility in the interior and Baixada Fluminense, strengthening his October candidacy and leveraging the favorable momentum of Bolsonarists in presidential polls.

item.title: 47,1% temem reeleição de Lula e 46,3%, eleição de Flávio
item.description: An AtlasIntel poll released on April 2, 2026 shows that 47.1% of Brazilians fear Lula's re-election more, while 46.3% fear Flávio Bolsonaro's election, indicating near-equal polarization. The survey also found that 57.4% of respondents perceive supporters of the candidate they most reject as 'manipulated/ignorant,' highlighting deep mutual distrust across political divides.

item.title: A razão da puxada de orelha de Rui Costa em Sidônio
item.description: Rui Costa reportedly pulled Sidônio Palmeira's ear in the ministerial meeting the day before yesterday at Lula's request, due to the president's dissatisfaction with the Secom minister's performance. The move was seen as a presidential signal amid low government approval in polls. The article is subscriber-only and requires registration for full access.

item.title: Paraná Pesquisas: Flávio Bolsonaro cresce no Ceará, mas segue quase 20 pontos atrás de Lula
item.description: Lula leads with 52.7% in the first round and 55.3% in the second round in Ceará, while Flávio Bolsonaro trails at 29.7% and 33.7%, respectively. Despite a modest 2.4-point gain for Bolsonaro since March, Lula’s lead remains between 17.8 and 23 points in the first round and 16.4 to 21.6 in the second, within the margin of error. Other candidates perform insignificantly, all below 2.4%.

item.title: AtlasIntel: 86,7% dizem já ter voto decidido para outubro
item.description: The AtlasIntel poll shows that 86.7% of Brazilians have already decided their presidential vote for October 2026, with 63.6% expressing enthusiasm. The survey, conducted March 16–23, 2026, interviewed 4,224 people with a 2-point margin of error and 95% confidence level.

item.title: A estratégia de Pacheco para emplacar Messias no STF
item.description: Senator Rodrigo Pacheco (PSB) plans to meet with Senate President Davi Alcolumbre to expedite the analysis of Jorge Messias' nomination to the Supreme Federal Court (STF) by President Lula. Pacheco aims to overcome Alcolumbre's resistance, who prefers Pacheco for the STF seat and learned of the presidential message only through the press, further straining their relationship. There is expectation that Alcolumbre will delay the nomination in the Senate's Constitution and Justice Committee (CCJ), prompting Pacheco to intervene politically to unblock the process.

item.title: As novas campanhas publicitárias do governo Lula
item.description: The Lula government plans to launch two advertising campaigns in the second half of April to improve its polling numbers. Coordinated by Secom, the initiative aims to bolster the president's image amid declining popularity. Full access to the content is restricted to subscribers of the outlet that published the report.

item.title: Após Alckmin e Tabata, PSB amplia refúgio para liberais pró-Lula com filiação de Tebet e Pacheco
item.description: The PSB is expanding its base by incorporating liberal Lula-allied leaders such as Simone Tebet and Rodrigo Pacheco, following the earlier inclusion of Geraldo Alckmin and Tabata Amaral. This strategy aims to strengthen the party as a refuge for government allies who do not wish to join the PT, maintaining relevance in the progressive camp amid the rise of Bolsonarism. The recruitment of figures from MDB, DEM, União Brasil, and PSD reflects a party realignment driven by current political circumstances.

item.title: As ideias do novo ministro da Fazenda
item.description: The new Finance Minister, Dario Durigan, announced that the upcoming household debt reduction program will include debt discounts, government guarantees, and be simpler than the Desenrola initiative. He noted that over 15 million people have been banked during the current Lula administration. Durigan also criticized the rhetoric of some young leaders as 'little republican,' reaffirming his commitment to fiscal rules, a free election, and acceptance of results as democratic essentials.

item.title: Política no STF
item.description: The nomination of Jorge Messias to the Supreme Federal Court (STF) is criticized for being based on personal loyalty to President Lula rather than judicial merit, as the requirement of 'notable legal knowledge' is no longer considered in the selection process. The article argues that this practice undermines the Senate’s approval ritual, turning it into a political favor regardless of the president’s ideological line.

item.title: Programa contra inadimplência sai em 10 dias, diz Durigan
item.description: Finance Minister Dario Durigan announced that the government will launch a new anti-delinquency program within 10 days, aimed at simplifying debt renegotiation and incorporating financial education to prevent recurring indebtedness. Unlike the 2023 Desenrola initiative, this program will involve direct negotiations with banks, fintechs, and credit operators, offering discounts and government guarantees (not subsidies) to reduce financial institutions' risk. Durigan also ruled out federal intervention in the Bank of Brasília and said reducing the IOF for low-income debtors is under analysis, though he initially opposes the measure.

item.title: Governo pagou menos de 1% das emendas que precisa quitar até junho pelo calendário aprovado na Lei do Orçamento
item.description: By the end of March, the Lula government had paid only R$102.3 million in mandatory amendments for 2026, equivalent to 0.6% of the R$17.3 billion required for the first half of the year. Payments were exclusively for SUAS-related actions, benefiting 160 individual amendments from deputies and senators, with the PL and Republicans receiving the largest shares.

item.title: Estratégia eleitoral de Lula esbarra no caso Master e expõe as armadilhas do autoengano
item.description: Lula met with ministers who will run in the October elections to energize his re-election campaign, urging them to 'go after' Flávio Bolsonaro and claiming his government has achieved 'infinitely more' than the previous one. Rui Costa, from the Civil House, presented a comparison between Lula and Bolsonaro, describing the current administration as a 'change from water to wine,' and criticized Sidônio Palmeira of Communications for not doing his job properly. The article is subscriber-only, requiring registration for full access.

item.title: Todo mundo odeia o Lula?
item.description: Lula faces political isolation in Congress and the market, yet maintains strong popular appeal through concrete policies like income tax exemptions for workers, the Pé-de-Meia program, and taxing the super-rich. Despite the lack of a competitive center in 2026, voters prioritize economic and quality-of-life issues over ideological clashes, which could benefit Lula if he doubles down on what is already perceived as effective. The real divide is not between conservatism and progressivism, but between traditional social values and demands for economic justice and material security.

item.title: Condecorada, chefe de hospitais e pediatra: conheça a trajetória da primeira general mulher no Exército brasileiro
item.description: Pediatrician Claudia Lima Gusmão Cacho became the first woman to reach the rank of general in the Brazilian Army, following a formal ceremony at the Army Club in Brasília where she received the general's sword and command baton. The promotion was signed by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) after a secret vote by the High Command.

item.title: Messias amplia apoio, mas ainda não tem votos para indicação ser aprovada em comissão do Senado; veja placar
item.description: Attorney General Jorge Messias has gained support in the Senate's CCJ for his STF nomination, securing at least ten favorable votes, but still falls short of the 14 needed for approval. Six senators have declared opposition, and eleven remain undecided or unresponsive, leaving the outcome uncertain. While backed by the government base and factions of MDB and PSD, resistance from Senate President Davi Alcolumbre and indecision among others continue to hinder consensus.

item.title: Fala de Lula sobre senadores pensarem 'que são Deus' amplia desafio de Messias para ter maioria no CCJ
item.description: Lula stated that senators 'think they are God' in an interview, heightening tensions with Congress, particularly following prior strains with Senate President Davi Alcolumbre. The remark came on the same day Jorge Messias' nomination to the STF was formally submitted, though he still lacks sufficient votes for approval in the CCJ, despite gaining supporters since last year. The comment caused discomfort among Centrão leaders, complicating efforts to secure a majority for the nomination.

item.title: Grupo de Paes no PSD articula candidatura à presidência da Alerj e tenta ampliar bancada
item.description: The Eduardo Paes-aligned faction within the PSD is positioning to launch its own candidate for president of the Alerj and aims to grow its caucus from six to ten deputies, seeking to become the second-largest bloc in the assembly. The party is negotiating the affiliation of figures such as Célia Jordão, Vinicius Cozzolino, Renato Miranda, and André Correa, while considering Vitor Júnior and Correa as potential leaders. The final decision awaits the STF ruling on April 8, after which the PSD will confirm its stance.

item.title: Ministros do STF se mobilizam por Messias; aliados de Alcolumbre veem risco de derrota para Lula
item.description: STF ministers are mobilizing to approve Jorge Messias's nomination to the Supreme Court, viewing it as strategically important to prevent post-electoral instability and strengthen the Court amid a vacant seat. Allies of Senate President Davi Alcolumbre maintain neutrality, fearing the opposition—led by Flávio Bolsonaro—could turn the confirmation into a political defeat for Lula with electoral consequences.

item.title: Brasil recluta a los bancos en su lucha para eliminar la deforestación en la Amazonia
item.description: The Brazilian federal government now requires banks to check, starting this week, whether rural properties applying for subsidized credit have a history of deforestation since 2019, using an official database. The measure supports the goal of completely eliminating deforestation in the Amazon by 2030, as pledged by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva. While banks support the initiative, agribusiness producers have opposed it, viewing it as an excessive restriction.

item.title: Grandes distribuidoras de combustíveis decidem ficar fora de programa de subvenção ao diesel
item.description: Brazil's largest fuel distributors have decided not to participate in the first phase of the diesel subsidy program launched by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva to counter the impact of the Iran war on fuel prices. The companies cited economic and operational concerns about the program's viability, which aims to curb price increases through direct subsidies to the sector.

item.title: Pocotó, pocotó
item.description: The text critiques the rise of Ronaldo Caiado as a new political option in Brazil, questioning whether he truly represents a redeeming 'third way' capable of overcoming polarization or merely another repetition of choosing between bad and worse options. It suggests the country may be paying the 'karma' of past generations for accepting figures like Caiado as saviors, despite his platform not being clearly defined in the excerpt. The content is presented as exclusive subscriber material, requiring registration for full access.

item.title: Visão do passado arrisca reeleição
item.description: President Lula attributes his government's low approval ratings to poor communication about its achievements, blaming both the press for not adequately reporting on government actions and the Presidency's formal channels for failing to inform the public. He suggests the media should function as an extension of the Official Gazette, a view described as mistaken. The article is available only to subscribers.
